tokens: Check off the good things I did.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: COAS is to the overhead window; DOI postburn report we'll get; helmets and gloves are on; Cabin Repress (cicuit breaker) is Closed, Suit Gas Diverter (valve) to Egress; Egress; Cabin Reg(ulators) A and B, Egress; and we're standing by for the Updata Link switch.
We're going to get earthrise in just a second.
You ought to have earthrise by now, Dick.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Garbled) be a while yet because I'm looking backwards.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: It's going to be right straight out there or what, I wonder?
I'm kind of guessing it is.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: maybe the 15-degree inclination's going to do something to it.
I just always imagined it was right in front of us.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I thought I saw it coming up.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Just click them (meaning Hasselblad photos) off.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: They haven't got us yet, Pete.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: But I'll go over on it and we'll make it ...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: You can get some more pictures if you want.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Pause) f:11, (One) 250(th of a second) is what we've been using, isn't it, Pete?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: That's got to be so fantastic.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: We read you loud and clear, and we just watched our first Earthrise, which was fantastic!
And we had a great DOI (Descent Orbit Insertion) burn.
The X residual was 0; Y was plus two tenths (0.2); and Z was minus six tenths (-0.6).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Copy your residuals: X, 0; Y, plus 2...(correcting himself) 0.2; and Z, minus 0.6.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: AGS residuals were plus 0.3, plus 0.1, and minus 0.6.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: We have (a signal from) your steerable (high-gain antenna) now.
Could you give us High Bit Rate?
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: What else do we need to give him?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Going to put this (possibly the Hasselblad) away.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Time to get down to business.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: That's going to be one that's going to hang around for years.
I don't give a shit if it's ...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Okay, I'm going to go ahead and get my...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Hey, I didn't give them DATA.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (on-board): No they didn't ask for it yet.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: And, Houston, whenever you're ready, we're ready to give you P00 (Program Zero-Zero) and Data.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: See a pen floating around here anywhere, Pete?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: But I got to get the Updata Link first.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Wait - wait - don't select Inverter 1 yet, Pete.
Have you got your Inverter 1 circuit breaker off?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Coming over to AC and I'm going to Inverter 1.
That's a - that's a bad one, boy.
You could flip that out so easy.
I've been worried about that one for months.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Okay, let's get the AELD's closed and Abort Stages closed.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I don't think we ought to do that.
Cycle the CWEA(Caution and Warning Electronics Assembly).
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I never used the Stop button.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Would you put your Biomed Switch (to the ) Left (position)?
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: Had a feeling they were going to want that.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: How soon before you're going to send up the data, Houston?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: We're standing right by to do the mode-2 lock on the radar, so we're holding.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I've got a Rev 15 map update for you.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: That's where I'm holding right now.
I don't want to use the computer.
I'm waiting for them to uplink.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: In 5 minutes, you got to put on the Batt's.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Let's see if this 30 minutes works out.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: You tell me what time 30 minutes more is.
And I'll tell you if that's right.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: We got it (in) P00 and Data for you.
(To Al, telling him to set the switch to Data) Go ahead.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Long Pause) (To Carr) You're (that is, Earth is) about 30 degrees above our horizon now, Houston; and you're about a one-third crescent moon, and you really are beautiful.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Pause) We put on our Sunday best for you.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: We're pretty well suited out up here ourselves.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Boy, this thing sure flies nice.
(Long Pause) Hey, does it still look like we're 5 miles north (of our pre-flight, planned ground track)?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Okay, what kind of azimuth with respect to the Sun will I have on that?
Is it that little enough that I'm not going to notice it?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: The closer I get, the more I feel like it's just like landing on Earth.
Just let it fly down and park it somewhere.
We'll bound out and grab a rock.
(Pause) Let me read the PDI rules to you.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: No ignition, delay 5 seconds; then set Descent Engine Override, On.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: When I went to Voice Backup, it whistles.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: When I went (means 'put') my updata link to Voice Backup, it made a whistle on our Comm, so I'm just going to leave it off.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: What can I do to help you, Pete?
Let me cross them off for you.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Now that you got a new RLS (Reference Landing Site), we've got a new address 231 for you.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: (To Gordon) Coming at you with the radar, Dick.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: That whistle you heard, we think, was because we were still commanding.
They'd like to have you try it again.

tokens: Just tried it; it looks good on the Voice Backup here.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: And we've got mode-2 (radar) lock (on the Command Module).

tokens: ready for an altitude check as soon as you can give it to me.

tokens: Your chart goes that low, huh?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: All right, I'll give you one at 37 minutes.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: What's it going to be, roughly?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Looks like it's going to be around 290 to 295.

tokens: 295 would put us about 56 thou(sand foot perilune).
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: It's not going to be that high.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Maybe that's that type where you can't ...
Just screw it (meaning, forget it); who cares.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I don't think that's worth a damn, anyhow.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Give me one at every minute.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Houston, our first perilune altitude check, using the
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (To Houston) Do you concur or not (with the 57,500 foot perilune)?

tokens: Got another one (meaning another altitude readout).

tokens: Could you send me a good LM state vector, please?
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: What we got to do, Pete?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (To Houston) Second check shows it to be 59,000 feet.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: We think it probably won't do you much good to have it right now.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: Clipper and Houston are clearing good.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Man, this suit is too tight.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: No, that's funny; you're going to love it.
You're going to be like a bunny rabbit.
S-band antenna better be grabbing that baby.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: We're still looking at a perilune of 50,000.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: (To Carr) Our third one shows it's going to be 64,000.
It's just making kind of a sweep up.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: So I got to go Verb 37, Enter; 63, Enter.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: We don't need to do any of those things.
(Doesn't) make a damn bit of difference.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Agreeing) No, I'm going to kill it right now.

tokens: Rendezvous Radar to Slew; Slew, two of them, are open.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Okay, I show 19 minutes and 36 seconds.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Do you want me to set it?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Long Pause) 41; that tells me that there's exactly 19 minutes to go.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: See, it doesn't look any worse down low than it does high up.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (If) you got enough, I'll go back to P00.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: How about a little burn abort rules, babe?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I've already given them to you.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Slew make sure those bastards are open.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: And the landing radar, how's it doing?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I didn't turn it on yet; it's ...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Now let me read them to you.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Reading) "Delta-H within limits greater than 10 seconds and not out of limits greater than 60.
Beta good at greater than 10 grand (meaning 'greater than 10 thousand')."
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Reading) "If no throttledown by P64 plus 15, abort.
(Garbled) fuel 1 minute 34 seconds after a low level (warning) or when fuel quantity less than 2, unless landing imminent." Well, you know that.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Reading) "For flashing Landing Radar, Altitude, or Velocity lights that are preceded by a steady LR Light, cycle (meaning 'turn on, then turn off') the Radar Test Switch." Okay.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: The hell we aren't getting low.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Don't think about it, it's easier.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Pause) Hope your pulse is lower than mine.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: It's not too bad right at the moment.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: We look real good up here, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: On this Sun angle, or Sun azimuth, as far as we can see, there'll be no noticeable difference, as far as you're concerned.
All you got to do is watch out and not fly through any clouds.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: I'll stay away from the clouds.
And we're looking for the Snowman.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: 45 minutes, perilune altitude checks, Rendezvous Radar CB (circuit breaker), for the 99th time, they're both open (laughter).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Put your program alarm card out here and your ...
Slip that under your thing so you can honk that baby out (meaning 'have it ready to grab'), if we need it.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Man, there's some mighty big mountains out there.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I was just thinking about that while ago.
We'll shoot right between a couple of them.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I sure hope you have us lined up right, Houston, because there sure is some big mountains (laughing) right in front of us, right now.
I hope we go down in the middle.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Smoke over your circuit breakers while we wait.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Now they're all looking good, Pete.
Okay, my camera's ready to start.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: That's right; you can't land if you don't get down there amongst them.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: This must be Fra Mauro we're going over.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: We're looking at lines, right now.
We don't have our map up.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: (Laughter) There's nothing but rocks out there.
(Pause) I hope we go a lot further on.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: We do; we got a long ways to go.
Got a - hundreds of miles after PDI, for Christ's sake.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: We've done this so many times, we'd better do it right.
Don't let it get out of sight, don't get low, and don't try to fly too hard.
I'd like to fly it there on LPD.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: This baby (meaning the LM Guidance Computer) can save gas for us.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I'll try to talk about as much as I do in the doggone simulator.
If it looks like the gas is good, I'll say it; if it looks like we're too high or something, I'll say it, but I don't ...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: You've probably just passed over Theophilus (which is a very large crater centered at about 27E and 11S).
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: Tell us when you want us to turn on the mirror.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: You can turn the mirror on; give me the fox-corpen, and we got the hook down.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Interestingly enough, Houston, at this attitude, I still can see the lunar horizon.
I have to really peer to find it, but I can see it.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: And in 40 seconds we'll be going to P63.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Give me a Verb 47 when you can.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Long Pause) Did you give me a Verb 40, Noun 20?

tokens: You get the landing radar on your final trim.

tokens: Ready for the DPS (Descent Propulsion system) card?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: CB(11): DECA A - Gimbal AC, closed.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: CB(11), (16): Stab(ilization)/Control circuit breakers, all closed.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Attitude Control, three of them, Mode Control.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: You're going to have to speak louder; I don't know what's the matter.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: We can turn it (the DPS card?) over, can we?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Wait, let me look; let me look.
DPS to Tig; got to do that at 59 ...

tokens: Tie her down good; we couldn't lose that; we'd be sunk.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Standing by on the watch (probably the Digital Event Timer).
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Come up on VOX (voice activiated comm).
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Houston reads you loud and clear.

tokens: We begin our descent in 35 seconds.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: 10, 9, 8 - we have ullage - 7, 6, 5 - Pro...(Pause) I have Ignition.

tokens: Throttle up at 26 (seconds into the burn; that is, at 110:21:06).

tokens: (To Al) I can hardly hear you for some reason.

tokens: It really feels good (garbled under Carr).
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Pause) DPS (Descent Propulsion System) is looking good, Pete.

tokens: (Pause) There's a little RCS (Reaction Control System) activity, not too much.

tokens: 5208 (feet per second horizontal velocity), minus 20 (fps vertical).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Looking good at 2 (minutes into the burn).
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: MSFN agrees with PGNS and AGS.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: Feels good to be standing up in a g-field again.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: 4276 (feet per second horizontal velocity) minus 53 (fps vertical velocity) at 44,700 (feet altitude).

tokens: We'll even give them ED Batts today.

tokens: Yeah, don't forget the ED Batts today.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: About 6 feet per second low on
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Roger, you're looking good at three (minutes).

tokens: (Pause; With pleasure in his voice) I have an altitude light out and a velocity light out.

tokens: How's it look to you, Houston?

tokens: Let me know when it converges.
I'm going back to my normal displays.

tokens: And ED Batts are Go, Houston.

tokens: Okay; smoke over all the gauges.

tokens: Partial pressure CO2 (in the cabin) is it's usual zero.

tokens: (We) maneuvered a bit on that one.

tokens: Yeah, I'm getting a fair amount of RCS firing.
More than I think I should.

tokens: There's a five minute hack, Al.

tokens: Boy, it's really giving her heck on the RCS.

tokens: It (the RCS) really is banging it (the LM) around, isn't it?

tokens: Just gave you a little AGS update.

tokens: According to this here computer, it's right on the money.

tokens: Better turn that sequence camera on in a moment.

tokens: (When it) throttles down; I'm going to put on the camera.

tokens: PGNS and AGS just agree something fantastic!
(Pause) (6:)23, let's give it (the AGS) another update.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I can just barely see the horizon.

transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Why don't I go ahead and put that camera on now?

tokens: We're out of 19,000 feet; I've got some kind of a horizon out there; I've got some craters, too, but I don't know where I am, yet.

tokens: Let's smoke over the numbers at 7:30.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: 7:30, 1153 (fps horizontal velocity), not too bad, minus 135 (fps vertical velocity) .
We're descending a little faster than normal, and we're a little bit low (in altitude).

tokens: We'll be there in a minute.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Okay; passing 12,000 feet, according to our
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Spring-loaded to go grab that Surveyor.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I'm trying to cheat and look out there.
I think I see my crater.

tokens: Right down the middle of the road!!!
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Coming down at about 99 feet a second.
Got 15 percent fuel (remaining); I'll reset my watch (garbled under Carr).
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Okay; I'm going to click over one.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: That's so fantastic, I can't believe it!
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: The boys on the ground (meaning the trajectory experts who have them right on target) do okay.
1800 feet up, 39 degrees; you got 94 seconds of
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I want to move (the landing point) forward (west) a little bit.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: A thousand feet coming down at 30 (feet per second).
(Glancing out the window, again) Looks good out there, babe.

tokens: I got to get over to my right (north).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: You're at 330 feet, coming down at 4.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: 300 feet (altitude), coming down at 5 (feet per second).
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Look at that crater; right where it's supposed to be!
257 feet, coming down at 5; 240 coming down at 5.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Going to get some dust before long.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: 50 feet, coming down; watch for the dust.

tokens: Coming down at 2, Pete; you got plenty of gas, plenty of gas, babe.

tokens: I'll cycle the valves; you got your Descent Engine Command Override, Off?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I'll cycle the main shutoff valve.

tokens: You get both Reg(ulator)s pushed, babe.

tokens: Okay, I'll smoke over (that is, look over) the ascent (stage systems).

tokens: (I'll) get this book (the checklist) turned over.

tokens: Okay, we're in hot shape, Houston.

tokens: I got the 413 in and cycled the
transcript.speaker: Bean

Master Arm, have you turned it off?

tokens: Just watch the systems and stand by for a few minutes.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I think we're in a place that's a lot dustier than Neil's.

transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Boy, am I glad I was high, Al, I couldn't...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Holy cran, it's beautiful out here!
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: It sure is; it's something else.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I'll turn this camera off, Pete.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Bean (on-board) I'll check Descent Reg pressure, Pete.

tokens: I got a Go for T1.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Hey, we flew right by the crater, Houston, but this ground (to the west of the LM) looks neat out here.
We're not going to have any trouble going back there (to Surveyor Crater).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: We're in lunar alignment, Houston, with the
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Where'd you put her down, Pete; over on site 4?
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: About halfway between site 4 and site 3.

transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (To Houston) You guys did outstanding targeting!
I'll tell you, that thing was right down the middle.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Oh; we're glad to hear that, Pete.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: I'll tell you; it's a real pleasure for me to ride with a number 1 aviator.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Pause) Give me the time for P12.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Set the DET (Digital Event Timer) up.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: PGNS and AGS both looking great.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: I can't wait to get outside.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Snowman stood out so clear I couldn't believe it.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: It's a nice place to land.

transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I'm sorry I flew by, but I was just going too fast.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: It felt like you hit just a little bit on your back (footpad)...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: It's a good thing we leveled off high...

tokens: ...and came down, because I sure couldn't see what was underneath us once I got into that dust.

tokens: Look at those boulders out there on the horizon, Pete.

tokens: Those rocks...Are we on the Copernicus Ray area?

tokens: You're Stay; and if you'd like to recycle and try it again, we'll talk to Sims.

tokens: We're still mad at him (meaning the head of the simulations team) for earlier in the week.

tokens: Let's get off VOX (voice activated comm).

tokens: Say, Houston, are you watching our descent (helium pressure) Reg(ulator)s?

tokens: The S-He's holding fine; and the (descent stage helium) venting's going along well.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: We're going to start hustling along here, so I'd appreciate it if you'd give me a holler when it gets down in the 2 to 8 (psi) range.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: You can close your ox(idizer) vent.
Leave the fuel open for a little while.
transcript.speaker: Carr
